hypothesis_en: Mr President, I am speaking because I was alluded to in this debate about my abstention on 11 March on the occasion of the oral amendment which proposed that we should dedicate 11 March as the European International Day against Terrorism, and I have to clarify that I am not against the existence of an International Day against Terrorism or a European Day against Terrorism. If I had been against terrorism, I would have voted against it, I would have abstained, because in justifying that statement there was a false element, a manipulative element, an element that deceived the European opinion about the authorship of those sad and horrible attacks. I have to remember here that Mr Aznar, the government of the People's Party, promised to end terrorism in five years, eight years have passed and now, instead of terrorism, we have two. The key is to respect the identity, culture and religion of others, and the key is for an equitable international legal order that we all respect and not just when it suits certain interests.
reference_en: thank you very much President (1.66) I= I'm taking the floor because I've been referred to in the course of this debate as to my abstention on the eleventh of March in the event of the oral amendment proposing that we should devote the eleventh of March to International Day Against Terrorism my abstention has been referred to here in this debate I have to clarify that I'm not against there being a a terrorism day or a or a day against terrorism if I were against I would have voted against I abstained because in the justification of that de= declaration there was something that was incorrect and was in fact manipulation and aimed at misleading European public opinion as to who was behind those sad attacks it also said it has also been said that in a period of twenty years the Basque government has not succeeded in doing anything against terrorism and I have to remind you here that Aznar of the PP promised to do away with terrorism in five years eight years have elapsed and now instead of one form of terrorism we're facing two (1.02) so we should start to take on board the notion that beating terrorism is not something you can do just with the military and just with police repression we have to begin to understand that the key is to tackle injustice and poverty the key is to respect the identity culture and religion of others and another key has to do with a an equitable international setup with respect for all which we should all respect all the time and not just when it suits our certain certain interests thank you President
source_es: muchas gracias señor Presidente yo intervengo por haber sido aludido en e= en ste debate sobre mi abstención el pasado once de marzo con ocasión de la enmienda oral que proponía que el= dedicáramos como día internacional europeo como día europeo contra el terrorismo el once de marzo y tengo que aclarar que yo no estoy en contra de que exista un día internacional contra el terrorismo ni un día europeo contra el terrorismo si hubiese estado en contra había votado negativamente me astuve porque en la justificación de esa declaración se introducía un elemento falso un elemento manipulador un elemento que engañaba a la opinión europea con respecto a la autoría de esos e= tristes y horrorosos atentados también se ha aludido a que el Gobierno vasco en veinte años no ha p= sabido atajar el terrorismo yo tengo que recordar aquí que el Señor Aznar el gobierno del Partido Popular prometió acabar con el terrorismo en cinco años han pasado ocho años y ahora en lugar de un terrorismo tenemos dos y es que debemos de empezar a comprender que atajar el terrorismo no es posible sólo con medidas militares y policiales que lo que tenemos que hacer que la clave está en atajar la injusticia y la pobreza la clave está en respetar la identidad la cultura y la religión de los demás y la clave está por un ordenamiento jurídico internacional equitativo que respetemos todos y no sólo cuando conviene a unos determinados intereses muchas gracias señor Presidente
